title: pseudopanax edgerleyi
intro: Pseudopanax edgerleyi, also known as the Pittosporum edgerleyi or Edgerley's kawakawa, is a small tree native to New Zealand. It belongs to the Pittosporaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain alkaloids such as kawakawa alkaloids and isoplatyphylline. The medicinal properties of Pseudopanax edgerleyi are believed to have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, offering health benefits such as pain relief and wound healing. Pseudopanax edgerleyi can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
